title: Need some clarification over here
wikitext:
[]: Need some clarification over here             Quote Post by Protodude » Sat Aug 02, 2008 11:47 pm Hey all, first time poster, long time lurker. Anyway, I just got done watching 1.01 and I'm a bit bewildered on a few things: -Shinji and the broken bridge: Was he really contemplating suicide? The line " enough, I'm going back to Misato ", was this directed at the agents or was Shinji consciously deciding to return on his own, regardless of the agents' presence? -The final scene: -Seele-01 speaks of "Apocrypha", "Generalization stage" and the "Books of Codes." No clue what any of this is suppose to mean, first time I've heard any of this. This is based off of the subs I viewed, the "AVP Asian Video" version, so I'm not sure how legit it is.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sun Aug 03, 2008 12:57 am I think he was indeed contemplating suicide for a brief moment. What he says about going back can be interpreted in both the ways you presented, I believe, but more to himself (in my view). Not sure about the last question. I think the 'new vocab' may be part of rebuild's yet to be explained mysteries? (sorta like the identity of the 'white giant' [in nerv and on the moon], kaworu, etc..)

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Aug 03, 2008 3:26 am I don't think he was contemplating suicide there, we still have that original scene on the cliff for that. The bridge was more like "There's no way out anyway, so might as well go back".

[]: Re: Need some clarification over here             Quote Post by V » Sun Aug 03, 2008 11:20 am Protodude wrote: Hey all, first time poster, long time lurker. Anyway, I just got done watching 1.01 and I'm a bit bewildered on a few things: -Shinji and the broken bridge: Was he really contemplating suicide?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 03, 2008 12:00 pm Shinji and the broken bridge: Was he really contemplating suicide?

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Aug 03, 2008 12:22 pm Again, I don't see why he'd contemplate suicide in the bridge scene, when we already have covered that in the cliff scene. There's no point for such repetition. It doesn't seem to me like he's standing there long enough to even think about it anyway: he arrives, is surprised/shocked at the sight of the bridge being out and makes up his mind to return. Also note that he's still standing in safe distance from the hole; in front of the warning signs (unlike the cliff, where he had climbed over the fence). Sailor Star Dust wrote: I mean, a CELLO (the instrument that Shinji plays) is playing during this scene, and regardless of Shinji's intentions I think it's meant to represent him/his melancholy state. (I feel like a fanwanker.)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 03, 2008 1:13 pm But him being melancholy isn't exactly a proof for him wanting to jump, is it?

[]:             Quote Post by Protodude » Sun Aug 03, 2008 2:13 pm Awesome, thanks guys. One last thing, am I alone to think the film had a 'happier tone' in contrast the show? I haven't watched the show in at least a year, but from what I remember, it was fairly gloomy compared to RoE Various scenes seemed really uplifting, especially scenes involving characters telling Shinji good job/good work, plus Misato's constant pep talk. EDIT: Am I wrong to think Shinji was overall stronger, less wimpy and showed indications of having a reason to "exist?" The emphasis Misato put on the 3I seemed to impact Shinji in ways that seemed non existant in the TV version. I got the impression from Yashima that Shinji REALLY did care about "everyone" and found his reason to exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Aug 03, 2008 2:32 pm I've heard the opposite: the beginning episodes had lots of humor interjected that definitley would seem out of place in the later episodes, such as Misato's crazy driving that runs on anime physics, or some of the scenes from the apartment, and in the movie they got cut. Now, I haven't seen the movie yet (waiting for an english translation), but that sounds more depressing a way to start the story.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 03, 2008 6:15 pm Protodude: Shinji was more angrier compared to his anime series counterpart with his personality, but this slight change isn't so different that he seems like a different character. (Compared to say Manga-Shinji's personality). One last thing, am I alone to think the film had a 'happier tone' in contrast the show?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 08, 2008 9:23 am JimmyWolk wrote: Again, I don't see why he'd contemplate suicide in the bridge scene, when we already have covered that in the cliff scene. There's no point for such repetition. It doesn't seem to me like he's standing there long enough to even think about it anyway: he arrives, is surprised/shocked at the sight of the bridge being out and makes up his mind to return.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 08, 2008 11:24 am I dunno why, but suicide WAS the first thing I thought of when I saw that scene (Though I'm not saying he wasn't just running away instead of having any deeper motivation; given Shinji's personalty that does make sense and all). Does the script actually say anything about the MIBs following him/Shinji knowing ALL along? I figured that he only found out they were around once he got to the bridge. And does that mean the MIBs were even following him when he climbed up that mountain/they were tracking him even in episode 4 which is why they finally caught up at Kensuke's wargame tent? Given that Nerv's security division or whatever they're considered wasn't the brightest in the series (they took a week to find Asuka and screwed up big time with a few other things), I don't see why that suddenly changed in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by zarwin » Sun Aug 10, 2008 11:24 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Given that Nerv's security division or whatever they're considered wasn't the brightest in the series (they took a week to find Asuka and screwed up big time with a few other things), I don't see why that suddenly changed in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Aug 11, 2008 5:31 am zarwin wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: Given that Nerv's security division or whatever they're considered wasn't the brightest in the series (they took a week to find Asuka and screwed up big time with a few other things), I don't see why that suddenly changed in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 20, 2008 4:58 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I dunno why, but suicide WAS the first thing I thought of when I saw that scene

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 14, 2008 3:21 pm Late reply is late @_@ Reichu wrote: You had also read a review that had preconditioned you towards that interpretation, no?
